description: An ancient Indian practice uniting breath, movement, and mindfulness. Builds flexibility, functional strength, and body awareness through progressive postures.

movements.name: Mountain Pose (Tadasana)

commonMistakes.description: Locking the knees rigidly rather than keeping a microbend, which compresses the joint.
Letting the chin jut forward instead of lengthening the back of the neck, disrupting spinal alignment.

movements.description: The foundational standing posture from which all other poses originate. Develops postural awareness, even weight distribution, and the mental focus required for yoga practice.

movements.name: Warrior I (Virabhadrasana I)

commonMistakes.description: Allowing the front knee to collapse inward rather than tracking over the second toe.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears when raising the arms, creating neck tension.

movements.description: A powerful standing lunge that opens the hips and chest while building leg strength and stamina. The raised arms and lifted gaze cultivate focus and a sense of expansiveness.

movements.name: Warrior II (Virabhadrasana II)

commonMistakes.description: Leaning the torso forward over the front leg rather than keeping it stacked directly over the hips.
Allowing the back foot to angle inward too much, which reduces hip opening and stability.

movements.description: A wide-legged standing pose with strong isometric leg engagement that develops hip and groin flexibility. The steady gaze over the front hand cultivates stillness and concentration.

movements.name: Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana)

commonMistakes.description: Rounding the upper back rather than externally rotating the shoulders to create a flat spine.
Forcing the heels to touch the floor by bending the knees, which removes the hamstring stretch.

movements.description: A full-body stretch that simultaneously strengthens the arms and decompresses the spine. One of yoga's most practiced poses, used both as a transition and a resting position.

movements.name: Tree Pose (Vrksasana)

commonMistakes.description: Placing the raised foot against the standing knee, putting lateral stress on the joint.
Fixing the gaze on something unstable or shifting it around, which disrupts the balance drishti.

movements.description: A single-leg balance pose that cultivates concentration and rooted stability. The grounded foot provides the foundation while the raised leg and arms form the canopy above.

movements.name: Cobra Pose (Bhujangasana)

commonMistakes.description: Pushing up with the hands and engaging the arms rather than using back strength, compressing the lumbar spine.
Crunching the neck back instead of lengthening through the crown of the head.

movements.description: A gentle backbend that opens the chest and strengthens the spinal extensors. Commonly practiced in sun salutation sequences as a counter to the forward-folding movements.

movements.name: Child's Pose (Balasana)

commonMistakes.description: Holding tension in the shoulders instead of letting them melt toward the floor.

movements.description: A restorative resting posture that gently stretches the hips, thighs, and lower back. Used as a recovery pose between demanding postures to allow the nervous system to settle.

movements.name: Bridge Pose (Setu Bandha)

commonMistakes.description: Allowing the knees to splay outward instead of staying hip-width and parallel.
Squeezing the glutes so hard that the lower back overextends and takes the load.

movements.description: A supine backbend that strengthens the glutes and hamstrings while opening the chest. Can be held statically or used dynamically as a gentle spinal mobilization warm-up.

movements.name: Triangle Pose (Trikonasana)

commonMistakes.description: Collapsing the torso toward the floor rather than rotating it open toward the ceiling, losing the lateral spinal lengthening that defines the pose.
Placing the bottom hand on the shin and pushing into it to prop the torso, which compresses the side body instead of creating space.

movements.description: A lateral stretching posture that lengthens the side body and opens the hip and shoulder simultaneously. Creates a clear triangular line from the grounded feet through the extended arms.

movements.name: Seated Forward Fold (Paschimottanasana)

commonMistakes.description: Rounding the lower back and tucking the pelvis to reach the feet, which stretches the lumbar fascia instead of the hamstrings.
Pulling aggressively on the feet to force depth, activating the stretch reflex and causing the hamstrings to contract protectively.

movements.description: A calming pose that stretches the entire posterior chain from heels to neck. Regular practice progressively lengthens the hamstrings and releases chronic tension in the lower back.

movements.name: Pigeon Pose (Eka Pada Rajakapotasana)

commonMistakes.description: Allowing the pelvis to tilt toward the bent-leg side, which compresses the sacroiliac joint rather than opening the external hip rotators.
Sinking the torso weight into the hip passively without any active engagement of the core, which can overstretch the capsular ligaments of the hip joint.

movements.description: A deep hip opener targeting the piriformis and hip rotators with intensity. The grounded leg's external rotation creates a profound stretch for those who hold tension in the hips.

movements.name: Crow Pose (Bakasana)

commonMistakes.description: Looking straight down at the floor rather than forward, causing the center of gravity to shift back and dropping the hips.
Keeping the arms straight instead of maintaining a slight bend, which removes the shelf that the knees need to rest on.

movements.description: An arm balance requiring core engagement and the confidence to shift weight past the tipping point. Develops wrist strength and spatial awareness as the entry point to advanced arm balances.
